claim_reviews.claim: The ratio of Zaremba density for {1,k} to {2,k} follows an inverse-square law: amplification ≈ 1633/k² with R² = 0.991 and exponent −2.002.
claim_reviews.evidence: Only 8 data points (k=3–10) are fitted. Densities for the two alphabets are measured at different cut-offs (10¹¹ vs 10¹⁰), so the ratio may include finite-N bias.  R² close to 1 for 3 degrees of freedom is not difficult to obtain.  No error bars or replicate runs are given.  Literature contains no prior report of an inverse-square law, so independent checks are required.
claim_reviews.concerns: Re-compute both densities at the same N for multiple N; give standard errors of the fit.

claim_reviews.claim: Digit 1 amplifies density by 200× at k=3, decaying to 18× at k=10.
claim_reviews.evidence: The provided table shows the stated ratios.  Given the reported enumeration method, these numbers are internally consistent.

claim_reviews.claim: All densities were obtained by complete enumeration up to N (no sampling) on 8×NVIDIA B200 GPUs.
claim_reviews.evidence: The code is linked but not benchmarked in the manuscript.  Without a runtime analysis or memory-use description, the reviewer cannot confirm that a full enumeration to 10¹¹ denominators is feasible within GPU memory and time.
claim_reviews.concerns: Provide wall-clock times, GPU occupancy statistics, and verification against known enumerations (e.g. Hensley data up to 10⁹).

claim_reviews.evidence: A least-squares fit of log(amplification) versus log(k) for the eight reported points reproduces R²≈0.991.

claim_reviews.claim: The number of {1,2,k} exceptions grows roughly like 3.2ᵏ and satisfies log E(k) = −0.030k² + 1.73k −1.90 with R² = 0.997.
claim_reviews.evidence: Growth ratios are computed from eight empirical counts, half of which are still ‘open’ at 10¹¹.  No statistical or theoretical justification for an exponential-quadratic model is supplied.
claim_reviews.concerns: Provide larger k, error estimates, and compare with Hensley’s upper and lower bounds for restricted-digit sets.

claim_reviews.claim: The exception set for {1,3,5} is finite with an estimated limit of ≈81 005 exceptions.
claim_reviews.evidence: The data show a declining increment, but no mathematical argument guarantees eventual closure; the extrapolation is speculative.
claim_reviews.concerns: Demonstrate saturation at a higher N or supply a heuristic with quantified confidence.

claim_reviews.claim: GPU enumeration to N = 10¹¹ for a two-digit alphabet is feasible on the stated hardware.
claim_reviews.evidence: A two-digit continued-fraction tree up to depth log_φ N has ≈O(N) nodes; bit-set storage for 10¹¹ bits is 12.5 GB, within a B200’s 180 GB, but no runtime or I/O numbers are given.
claim_reviews.concerns: Include performance metrics and memory footprint; cross-validate a subset with CPU enumeration.

claim_reviews.claim: Digit 1’s dominance is ‘quadratically’ stronger than digit 2 because of the golden ratio structure.
claim_reviews.evidence: This is an interpretative statement with no derivation.  Existing theory (Gauss measure, Hensley dimension formula) does not predict a k⁻² factor.
claim_reviews.concerns: Either supply a theoretical argument or phrase the statement as conjectural.

overall_assessment: The empirical observations are intriguing and the data appear internally consistent, but key claims rely on a small sample and on computations that have not been independently benchmarked.  The inverse-square law may be a real phenomenon, yet the current evidence is not conclusive.  Strengthening the computational methodology section, aligning cut-offs, and adding statistical uncertainty would raise the work to a publishable standard.
recommendations: Recompute both {1,k} and {2,k} densities at the same N for several N (e.g. 10⁹, 10¹⁰, 10¹¹) to test stability of the exponent.
Release logs showing runtime, GPU utilisation, and hash checksums of bitsets to aid reproducibility.
Provide confidence intervals for densities and for the fitted exponent and intercept.
Clarify speculative language around the golden-ratio explanation and the finiteness of the {1,3,5} exception set.
Cite the missing references and discuss how the present findings relate to their bounds and methods.
certification_justification: The observation is novel and interesting but has not yet been corroborated by external studies or benchmarked against published datasets.
